author: Security Bot
message:
fix(auth): make DPoP replay cache check-and-insert atomic

The checkAndAddJTI: method performed a SELECT then INSERT without
a transaction, creating a TOCTOU race where concurrent requests
could both observe a JTI as unused and both succeed.

Fix: wrap the check-and-insert in BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION /
COMMIT so the second thread blocks until the first commits, then
sees the row and returns NO (replay detected).

Add 3 new tests: concurrent duplicate JTI rejection, nil JTI
handling, nil expiration handling.

Fixes MEDIUM finding from 2026-04-25 production readiness re-audit.

👾 Generated with [Letta Code](https://letta.com)

Co-Authored-By: Letta Code <noreply@letta.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
admin: create Category C stub templates for missing partials

Create 5 stub templates with empty-state placeholders for partials that had
no template or handler. These prevent blank content panes and provide a
foundation for future implementation.

Templates created:
- reports.html: Wrapper that lazy-loads reports/list
- reports_list.html: Empty state for report list
- audit_log.html: Empty state for audit log entries
- hosting_history.html: Empty state for hosting history
- ozone_correlations.html: Empty state with "Coming Soon" alert

Each uses the empty-state-strawberry styling to provide a consistent,
non-jarring user experience instead of blank panes.

Add if-cases in AdminPartialHandler.handlePartialNamed: to wire these partials
to their respective templates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
admin: fix users tab — use PDSController.getAllAccountsWithError instead of nil database stub

Replace the self.database path in handleAdminUsers: with a direct call to
[[PDSController sharedController] getAllAccountsWithError:]. The self.database
path was a stub returning nil, causing the admin UI PDS tab to always show 0
accounts. The working path routes through PDSAccountService → PDSLegacyAccountRepository
→ PDSServiceDatabases, which correctly retrieves accounts from the actor store.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
Fix admin UI login failures: JS ReferenceError, keychain signing crash, and 401 on partials

- Fix JS ReferenceError: map switchService to switchToService in window.AdminUI export (app.js:861)
- Fix Keychain private key lookup: add kSecAttrKeyClassPrivate filter to SecItemCopyMatching query (PDSAppleKeyManager.m:646)
  - On macOS, both public and private keys are stored in Keychain with the same application tag
  - Without the class filter, SecItemCopyMatching returns the public key (class=0) instead of private key
  - This caused OSStatus -50 when signing, which made admin authentication fail with nil token
- Fix hardcoded @"RS256" JWT header in signPayload:withKeyID: to use actual key algorithm (PDSAppleKeyManager.m:542)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
fix(appview): resolve CID links in DAG-CBOR record blocks

Add recursive CID link resolution in AppViewIngestEngine to follow IPLD
references in firehose CAR blocks. Firehose blocks often contain CID links
(CBOR tag 42) that reference other blocks containing the actual record data.

Changes:
- Add ResolveCIDLinksInObject() to recursively resolve CID references
- Prefer decodeDataAsJSON() for better CID->$link wrapper handling
- Use blockWithCID:() for direct lookup instead of looping
- Check for actual record fields instead of just non-empty dicts
- Add detailed debug logging for CID resolution tracking

This fixes the issue where graph records were appearing as empty dicts
because their actual data was in linked blocks that weren't being
followed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
fix(appview,sync): harden CAR parsing and implement persistent handle resolution

Summary:
- Refactored CARReader to use spec-compliant varint-based CID parsing.
- Updated CID.m to support CIDv0 and robust CIDv1 binary decoding.
- Updated AppView indexers to use provided CIDs and DAG-CBOR codec.
- Added persistent 'handles' table to AppViewDatabase for fast handle-to-DID resolution.
- Integrated handle discovery into firehose ingest and backfill worker.
- Verified 100% pass rate (1648/1648 tests).

author: Security Bot
message:
fix(relay): capture weak delegates strongly to prevent SIGBUS crashes

Relay was crashing with EXC_ARM_DA_ALIGN on ARM64 when weak delegate
properties were accessed in dispatch_async blocks without strong capture.
The delegate could be deallocated between block creation and execution,
causing an alignment fault when messaging garbage memory.

Fixed in RelayClient.m (6 instances):
- notifyDisconnectionWithError:
- firehoseSubscriptionDidConnect:
- firehoseSubscription:didReceiveCommitEvent:
- firehoseSubscription:didReceiveIdentityEvent:
- firehoseSubscription:didReceiveErrorEvent:
- firehoseSubscription:didCloseWithError:

Fixed in RelayUpstreamManager.m (6 instances):
- relayClient:didReceiveCommitEvent:
- relayClient:didReceiveIdentityEvent:
- relayClient:didReceiveErrorEvent:
- relayClientDidConnect:
- relayClient:didDisconnectWithError:
- relayClient:didReceiveCursor:

Pattern: Capture delegate strongly with local variable before async block
or before direct call, then check for nil before messaging.

Added debugging skill for future reference.

🤖 Generated with Letta Code (https://letta.com)

Co-Authored-By: Letta Code <noreply@letta.com>

author: Security Bot
message:
fix(relay): fix URL scheme detection for localhost upstreams

NSURL parses "localhost:2583" with scheme="localhost" which is wrong.
Now check for "://" presence first before URL parsing to correctly
detect missing scheme, then prefix with http:// or https://.
